rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltff'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS ADRIAN FOX, WILLIAM MCMULLEN, JR., SCOTT WINKLER's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatories 1 and 2 is granted in part and denied in part. The court grants Class Action plaintiffs' discovery of the names and addresses and Vin Numbers of putative class members. The court has weighed balancing the need for discovery with constitutionally protected privacy rights and imposes appropriate third-party privacy protections. See Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc. v. Super. Ct., 40 Cal.4th 360, 373 (2007) and Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athletic Assn. 7Cal. 4th 1 (1994). The privacy safeguard shall be a protective order limiting viewing of the names and addresses to attorney-eyes only. That portion of the plaintiff's motion seeking discovery of putative class members' email addresses and/or phone numbers is denied. The scope of the names and addresses of car owners who have not made a claim under warranty is "owners of subject vehicles equipped with power valve engine screws in the intake manifold". Plaintiffs' attempt to expand the definition to include [screws] "that are not locked tight or may be prone to loosening" is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs' failed to offer proof in their moving papers defendant is aware of which vehicles meet that added definition or defendant has that information. Plaintiff's supplemental filing also does not meet the necessary level of proof. Defendant's objection to the interrogatory is overbroad is sustained to the extent it seeks to include the expanded definition. Special Interrogatory 2 seeking to identify individuals who have filed a complaint shall be answered separately. The defendant's objection the information being sought was previously a topic of discovery is overruled. The parties shall "meet and confer" to work out the language of an appropriate protective order regarding the information to be provided in response to Special Interrogatories 1 and 2. Plaintiffs request for monetary sanctions is are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. WHITE SNOW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Plaintiff's Motion in the Alternative for Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to the cause of action for breach of contract against Defendant Martin Eng. It is undisputed that 1) there was a contract between White Snow Corporation and Plaintiff; 2) Eng is the owner of Defendant White Snow Corporation; 3) Eng agreed to be held jointly and severally liable for all amounts owed by White Snow in the event of default; 4) White Snow breached the contract; 5) Plaintiff did not breach the contract; and 6) White Snow owes to Plaintiff $35,601.93. (UMF # 1, 2.) Plaintiff is therefore entitled to judgment against Eng. The Motion is denied as to the cause of action for open book account because Plaintiff has failed to produce any evidence of a record constituting an open book. The Motion is also denied as to the cause of action for account stated. Plaintiff has failed to produce evidence that Eng promised to pay the amount stated. Furthermore, the opposition filed by Defendant Eng was untimely filed. However, even if the opposition was considered, the Court would not alter the ruling as Eng failed to raise any triable issues of material fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JI YANG et al VS. HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Deft'S Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Defendant's Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Motion denied. This Motion cites the same evidence as Defendant's original motion for attorneys' fees, and cites no new facts, circumstances, or law, as required under CCP section 1008(a). The Court relied on all evidence properly submitted with Defendant's original motion, and the failure of Defendant to provide to the Court additional evidence which it would have liked the Court to consider does not establish a "new circumstance." Furthermore, Defendant's request in the alternative that the Court grant the Motion for Reconsideration pursuant to CCP section 473(b) is denied as Defendant seeks relief not available under CCP section 473(b). That code provision permits relief from a prior order; it does not permit the Court to modify a prior order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HEATHER RODRIGUES et al VS. PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Preventing Defts From Depositing Pltff'S Counsel Ashley Baltazar And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Its Attorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF HEATHER RODRIGUES, STACY BALL-WEAVER, LAUARIE ANDESON Motion For Protective Order Preventing Defendants From Depositing Plaintiff'S Counsel Ashley Baltazar And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Attorney Of Record. (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for a protective order to prevent Defendants from deposing Plaintiffs' counsel Ashley Baltazar is GRANTED. Defendants have not shown "extremely good cause" to depose opposing counsel. See Carehouse Convalascent Hospital v. Superior Court, 143 Cal. App. 4th 1558, 1562 (2006) (explaining that depositions of opposing counsel are "presumptively improper"). The attorney verifications of the Plaintiffs' DFEH complaints are facially consistent with and authorized by the holdings of Blum v. Superior Court, 141 Cal. App. 418, 424 (2006) and Rickards v. United Parcel Service, Inc., 206 Cal. App. 4th 1523, 1529 (2012). Those cases do not support Defendants' assertion that Baltazar has verified the DFEH complaints based on her personal knowledge. Thus, even assuming that an attorney verification untruthfully claiming personal knowledge constitutes grounds to depose opposing counsel, no such grounds exist here. Accordingly, the information Defendants seek from Baltazar is not crucial to the preparation of this case. See Carehouse Convalascent Hospital, 143 Cal. App. 4th at 1563. Moreover, the information is subject to a privilege. Id. The Judge Pro Tem further finds that the motion was timely filed pursuant to Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2025.420(a), which permits a party to move for a protective order "[b]efore, during, or after a deposition." Here, Plaintiffs filed their motion prior to the deposition, and the meet and confer letters gave an impression that the deposition had already been taken off calendar in any event. Finally, Plaintiffs' request for sanctions in the amount of $3,340 IS GRANTED because Defendants did not act with substantial justification in connection with this motion. See Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2025.420(h). The cases on which Defendants rely - Blum and Rickards - do not support and, in fact, contradict Defendants' position. That, combined with the fact that depositions of opposing counsel are "presumptively improper," leads the Judge Pro Tem to conclude that sanctions are appropriate. (Continued to Part 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE FRED S. VS. NORMANDY APARTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Preliminary Injunction [To Be Heard Concurrently With Motion To Confirm Appraisal As Arbitration Award]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT NORMANDY APARTMENTS' Motion To Vacate Preliminary Injunction [To Be Heard Concurrently With Motion To Confirm Appraisal As Arbitration Award]. The motion is denied. Defendant waived the bond requirement. See Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729. The opposition to the motion for preliminary injunction did not raise the bond issue. At the hearing, defendant only sought a bond with regard to the disbursements and the court ordered no injunctive relief as to the disbursements (Reporters Transcript pgs. 11 and 17). The injunction was properly ordered as the only means to compel the appraisal process. Defendant's motion is an improper motion for reconsideration and/or motion to dissolve that does not meet the requirements of CCP 1008 and CCP 533. Defendant does not highlight what material changes have occurred to warrant a change in the injunction order. Defendant fails to provide guidance regarding how paragraph 3 of the injunction should be amended to streamline the appraisal process. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE FRED S. VS. NORMANDY APARTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Appraisal As Arbitration Award [To Be Heard Concurrently With Motion To Vacate Preliminary Injunction]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT NORMANDY APARTMENTS' Motion To Confirm Appraisal As Arbitration Award [To Be Heard Concurrently With Motion To Vacate Preliminary Injunction]. The motion to confirm the arbitration award is denied. The court previously rejected the waiver/forfeiture argument, defendant fails to show that plaintiff breached the partnership agreement or that any alleged breach was not excused, and par. 20 of the partnership agreement does not provide for forfeiture as a remedy. The appraisal process shall proceed forthwith. The court denies the request for fees without prejudice. If plaintiff believes he has a basis for fees, he should file a formal motion. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL B. HILLEGEIST VS. F/V SUSAN T. INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT PATRICK DAVIS, F/V SUSAN T. INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint sufficiently alleges that Plaintiff did not become aware of any injury until August 11, 2011. The cases upon which Defendants rely for the application of the "time of event rule" are all distinguishable in that each of those plaintiffs had identified some injury but delayed filing actions due to limited knowledge of the extent or cause of their injuries. Here, application of the discovery rule is appropriate because absent any knowledge of injury, Plaintiff could not have been on notice that he may have had a claim against the Defendants. However, the Complaint does not state sufficient facts for Plaintiff to rely on the discovery rule to delay the accrual of his claims. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 809.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RANDALL EVANS VS. COLLECTIVE IMPACT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 27, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT JAMES SPINGOLA's Motion To Stay Proceedings. Continued to November 13, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver complete courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 27, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Moving party only provided the first two pages of the Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
